overview: This Black/Red Modern deck utilizes fast, interactive spells to establish early board presence and apply relentless pressure. By combining efficient hand disruption with aggressive threats, it aims to close out games before opponents can stabilize. The strategy relies on precise resource management and leveraging tempo swings to maintain control of the board state.

keep_criteria: Hands with early interaction like Thoughtseize
Hands containing Moonshadow or Super Shredder
Hands with two lands and a one-drop
Keep hands with 2-3 lands, Ragavan, and at least one interaction (Thoughtseize, Fatal Push, or Lightning Axe).
Prioritize hands with Ragavan + discard over pure curve-balls.
mulligan_criteria: Hands with three or more lands
Mulligan hands with 3+ lands and no early plays, or hands with zero interaction when facing fast aggro.

early_game: Play lands and one-drops to establish board presence
Use Thoughtseize to disrupt opponent's key plays
Cast Moonshadow to apply immediate pressure
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Thoughtseize if you have a 2-drop threat or Ragavan in hand.
Turn 2: Ragavan into a land, or discard into a threat.
Sequence lands to ensure you can cast Ragavan on turn 2 and follow up with a cantrip or discard.
If facing fast decks, prioritize early interaction over optimal mana.
mid_game: Deploy Super Shredder to clear blockers and generate value
Maintain board control with efficient removal
Push damage while denying opponent's recovery
Ragavan's card draw fuels your hand
Use discard to protect your board and disrupt opponent's key pieces.
Manage mana to keep removal/discard available for 2-3 mana threats.
Commit Ragavan early to establish pressure and draw into answers.
Hold back if opponent has clear removal or a board wipe ready.
late_game: Leverage card advantage from early disruption
Use remaining resources to close out the game
Adapt to opponent's late-game threats with targeted answers
Ragavan + efficient threats (Moonshadow, Super Shredder, Bloodtithe Harvester).
Win by out-valuing through card advantage and applying consistent pressure.
When you have card advantage, opponent is low on removal, or you've established board control.
Go all-in if you can close the game before they can stabilize.

key_cards.name: Super Shredder
key_cards.role: Primary win condition and board clear
key_cards.notes: Use its activated ability to remove opposing creatures while dealing damage, then attack with it to close games quickly.

key_cards.name: Moonshadow
key_cards.notes: Cast on turn one to establish a flying threat that forces opponents to use removal or face early damage.

key_cards.name: Thoughtseize
key_cards.notes: Target key threats or combo pieces in the opponent's hand to delay their game plan and protect your board.

sequencing_tips: Hold Thoughtseize for the opponent's most impactful card
Cast Moonshadow before playing lands to maximize surprise
Use Super Shredder's ability to clear blockers before attacking
Common mistake: Overextending with Ragavan without protection.
Another: Forgetting that Super Shredder and Moonshadow synergize well with artifact synergies if the deck runs any, but even without, they're efficient.
Ragavan's value is lost if wiped without follow-up.
Keep discard flexible to hit different strategies.

matchups.advice: Apply early pressure with Moonshadow and use Thoughtseize to find their win conditions.
Use Thoughtseize to remove their key threats and stabilize with Super Shredder.
Race them by deploying threats quickly and using disruption to slow their value engines.
Use discard to remove counterspells. Cast Ragavan and threats in response to or after they tap out. Use cheap, efficient threats that are hard to counter (or cast them when they're tapped out).
Early interaction is key. Thoughtseize to find Amulet or ramp spells. Use removal to kill early threats. Super Shredder can clear artifacts.
Win the discard war. Prioritize keeping hands with multiple threats and interaction. Use Ragavan to out-value. Sideboard in more discard and efficient removal.

common_threats: Watch for counterspells (Jeskai), artifact acceleration (Affinity/Amulet), and combo win conditions (Ruby).
Key tells: opponent tapping out on turn 2/3, holding up mana, or playing non-basic lands that signal specific strategies.
